parsed_review.summary: CP00-914 is a descriptor-only, risk-class-A closeout pack bridging the final RP30.P02.M06 RuleEngine tail row (S12) into nine RP30.P02.M07 LeaveWorkflow re-entry rows (S01-S09), 10 units total, matching the baseline distribution {RuleEngine:1, LeaveWorkflow:9}. Source inspection across packages/hrx/src/registry.js (HRX_CP914 binding/requirements/section-rows, case set, descriptor, Hermes/Claude packets, closeout handoff, contract projection), packages/hrx/src/validators.js (coverage + descriptor validators, validateHrxPeopleContract pinned to CP00-914), contracts/hrx-people-contract.json (latest_pack/latest_projection HrxCp914RuleEngineLeaveWorkflowBridgeDescriptor sourced from HrxCp913RuleEngineTailDescriptor, validation.valid=true), scripts/validate-rp30-hrx-people-contract.mjs (CP913->CP914->CP915 chain), README, and packages/hrx/test/model.test.js (3 tests: binding/coverage, descriptor runtime-closed, contract projection) shows a consistent, well-tested implementation. Descriptor-only boundaries are enforced in code and asserted by tests: no rule definitions/execution-runtime/decision-writes, no leave request payloads/workflow-runtime/approval-writes/policy-rule-execution, no payroll runtime, no HR AI final judgment, no permission/audit/product-state writes, no runtime receipts, no separate HRX product, no real employee/candidate/payroll/document data, no credentials/secrets (grep for real-data/secret patterns returned none). Authority boundary is correctly preserved: Claude is read_only non-final reviewer (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), human approval is required for runtime opening (CP00-915), and local validation does not claim enterprise trust. Hermes packet is static metadata with emits_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic review/evidence handling, not product runtime). Review readiness auth preflight passed (stdout_bytes=1228) and the claude-review-result/construction-inspection/adjudication PENDING state is the expected pre-promotion template that this review gates, not stale/invalid evidence. Reported HRX command exit codes (node --check, node --test, rp30:hrx/rp30/hrx:requirements/hrx:weighted validate) are all 0. No P0/P1/P2 issues; one P3 provenance note recorded. Does not block pack or this CP closeout.

findings.title: Full deterministic validation ladder and adjudication/inspection are deferred post-review steps not re-executable under read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records only the HRX-scoped commands (node --check, node --test packages/hrx/test/*.test.js, rp30:hrx:validate, rp30:validate, hrx:requirements:validate, hrx:weighted:validate), all exit_code 0. The broader closeout ladder named in the prompt (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) plus adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and claude-review-result.json are still in their PENDING templates and run during the post-review promotion step. As a read-only reviewer (Read/Grep/Glob only, no Bash) I confirmed these via direct source/artifact inspection and reported exit codes, but did not independently re-execute them. This matches the documented gate sequence (command-evidence note: 'Full hardened read-only Claude review, adjudication, production_ready promotion, queue regeneration, full deterministic validation ladder, and commit are required before production_ready') and the established CP00-897..913 closing pattern.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with promotion only after the full deterministic validation ladder and git diff --check complete cleanly and adjudication/inspection/claude-review-result are populated from this receipt; no code change required. Provenance distinction (directly inspected vs reported exit codes) is noted for audit transparency.
